perturbation.original_text: Each of Photronics and DNP is hereinafter referred to as a "Shareholder" and collectively as the "Shareholders", each of the Shareholders and PDMC is hereinafter referred to as a "Supplier" and collectively as the "Suppliers", and each of the Suppliers and the Company is hereinafter referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties." ARTICLE 1. BACKGROUND Photronics and DNP wish to participate in a joint venture, either directly or indirectly through their respective Affiliates, as equity interest owners in the Company, and to carry on the Business through the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of Photronics and DNP is hereinafter referred to as a "Shareholder" and collectively as the "Shareholders". For the purposes of liability under Article 8 (Indemnification), a 'Shareholder' shall mean only those parties holding at least 25% equity interest in the Company. each of the Shareholders and PDMC is hereinafter referred to as a "Supplier" and collectively as the "Suppliers", and each of the Suppliers and the Company is hereinafter referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties." ARTICLE 1. BACKGROUND Photronics and DNP wish to participate in a joint venture, either directly or indirectly through their respective Affiliates, as equity interest owners in the Company, and to carry on the Business through the Company.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradictory definition of "Shareholder". Initially, any equity holder is a "Shareholder". The added sentence restricts the definition of 'Shareholder' for the limited purpose of indemnification under Article 8 to only those holding at least 25% equity. This contradicts the general definition of Shareholder which includes all equity holders, creating ambiguity on the rights and responsibilities of a Shareholder, particularly regarding indemnification responsibilities. It creates uncertainty in **Article 8**, as it's not clear who will fall under the indemnification clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 8. INDEMNIFICATION 8.1 Indemnification by the Suppliers Each of the Suppliers shall, with respect to Products supplied by such Supplier, defend, indemnify and hold harmless the Company from and against any third party claims, expenses and costs (including but not limited to attorney and other professional fees and expenses), settlement (if negotiated and approved by the relevant Supplier), damages and liability to the extent arising from a claim (a) alleging that a Product infringes or misappropriates any Intellectual Property Rights, or (b) arising under products liability theory from a manufacturing defect, and shall pay any judgments finally awarded by a court or any amounts contained in a settlement agreed to by the relevant Supplier arising from such claims. The foregoing indemnity does not cover claims that solely arise from (i) the modification of the Product by any party other than the relevant Supplier, (ii) the combination or use of the Product with other products, processes, methods, materials or devices except as approved by the relevant Supplier, or (iii) the fault of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradictory definition of "Shareholder" within the contract. The original text and the contradicted text (Article 8) refer to "Shareholders" without qualification. The changed text then introduces a new, restricted definition of 'Shareholder' *solely* for the purposes of Article 8 (Indemnification), limiting it to those holding at least 25% equity interest. This directly contradicts the broader definition elsewhere in the contract and creates ambiguity about who qualifies as a "Shareholder" for indemnification purposes under Article 8, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty.

The Suppliers shall notify the Company of acceptance or rejection of a [***]within [***]hours of receipt of a [***]. Failure of the Suppliers to accept or reject a [***]within [***]hours shall constitute acceptance of such [***]. The lead time for the Products will be as set forth in the applicable [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 Purchase Orders The Suppliers will use commercially reasonable efforts to accept all [***]from the Company that comply with this Agreement including adhering to all relevant specifications of the Product as set forth in the [***]entered into between the Company and the Supplier (including the [***] (as defined below)). However, the Suppliers are only required to accept Purchase Orders up to a maximum value of $[***] per quarter. The Suppliers shall notify the Company of acceptance or rejection of a [***]within [***]hours of receipt of a [***]. Failure of the Suppliers to accept or reject a [***]within [***]hours shall constitute acceptance of such [***]. The lead time for the Products will be as set forth in the applicable [***].
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence introduces a contradiction regarding the Supplier's obligation to accept purchase orders. Initially, the suppliers are required to use commercially reasonable efforts to accept all orders. The new condition limits this obligation to a maximum value of $[***] per quarter. This contradicts the initial obligation, which suggests an unlimited quantity if the order is in compliance with the agreement. This creates uncertainty in **Section 2.4** regarding the Supplier's ability to supply Product, particularly when the total orders exceed the maximum value.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Rescheduling and Cancellation The Company may not [***]any portion of an accepted [***]unless the Supplier fails to fulfill any material term of such accepted [***]. The Suppliers shall at all times use prudent material planning practices, including by way of example, [***]. The Company [***]will be provided on a [***] basis covering a rolling [***]period. The Company will provide the Suppliers with such [***]which will be updated [***] and [***]which will be updated [***] and will be used for planning purposes only. If a Supplier's ability to supply any Product is constrained for any reason, such Supplier shall immediately notify the Company of such supply constraint for the purpose of resolving the same.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 2.2 introduces a limitation on the Supplier's obligation to accept purchase orders, capping it at a maximum value of $[***] per quarter. This directly contradicts the initial statement requiring 'commercially reasonable efforts to accept all [***] from the Company that comply with this Agreement.' The contradiction is further highlighted when considered in conjunction with Section 2.4. Section 2.4 states that the Company may not [***] any portion of an accepted [***] unless the Supplier fails to fulfill any material term of such accepted [***].' With the change in Section 2.2, if the supplier rejects purchase orders exceeding the quarterly value, it directly impacts the implications in Section 2.4 and creates a constraint on the Supplier's ability to supply Product. Thus, it impacts the Supplier's obligation to supply product, creating an ambiguity and conflict between the sections.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the Suppliers warrants that the Products shall comply with the specifications and documentation agreed by the relevant Supplier and the Company in writing that is applicable to such Products for the Warranty Period. This warranty does not apply to any Product failures resulting from misuse, storage in or exposure to environmental conditions inconsistent with those specified in the applicable specifications or documentation, modification of the Product by anyone other than the relevant Supplier. If a Product fails to comply with the foregoing warranty, the relevant Supplier shall, at its option, either [***]such Product, or, in the event the foregoing options are not commercially practicable, [***]to the Company any amounts paid for the applicable Product.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Suppliers warrants that the Products shall comply with the specifications and documentation agreed by the relevant Supplier and the Company in writing that is applicable to such Products for the Warranty Period. However, with respect to products sold to customers outside of Asia, the suppliers make no warranties whatsoever. This warranty does not apply to any Product failures resulting from misuse, storage in or exposure to environmental conditions inconsistent with those specified in the applicable specifications or documentation, modification of the Product by anyone other than the relevant Supplier. If a Product fails to comply with the foregoing warranty, the relevant Supplier shall, at its option, either [***]such Product, or, in the event the foregoing options are not commercially practicable, [***]to the Company any amounts paid for the applicable Product.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradictory condition to the limited warranty. Initially, the suppliers warrant that the Products comply with the specifications for the Warranty Period. This warranty is then negated with the addition of a sentence stating that for products sold to customers outside of Asia, the suppliers make no warranties whatsoever. This contradicts the initially stated warranty, creating ambiguity about the scope and applicability of the warranty provisions. This affects **Article 8 (Indemnification)** as a defect on product that is shipped outside of Asia may not fall under warranty and thus indemnification may be complicated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 8. INDEMNIFICATION 8.1 Indemnification by the Suppliers Each of the Suppliers shall, with respect to Products supplied by such Supplier, defend, indemnify and hold harmless the Company from and against any third party claims, expenses and costs (including but not limited to attorney and other professional fees and expenses), settlement (if negotiated and approved by the relevant Supplier), damages and liability to the extent arising from a claim (a) alleging that a Product infringes or misappropriates any Intellectual Property Rights, or (b) arising under products liability theory from a manufacturing defect, and shall pay any judgments finally awarded by a court or any amounts contained in a settlement agreed to by the relevant Supplier arising from such claims. The foregoing indemnity does not cover claims that solely arise from (i) the modification of the Product by any party other than the relevant Supplier, (ii) the combination or use of the Product with other products, processes, methods, materials or devices except as approved by the relevant Supplier, or (iii) the fault of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a significant contradiction. The original text establishes a warranty for all products, while the changed text negates this warranty for products sold outside of Asia. This directly conflicts with Article 8, which outlines indemnification based on product defects. If there's no warranty for products sold outside Asia, the indemnification clause becomes unclear and potentially unenforceable for those products, as a manufacturing defect might not trigger the indemnification obligations. This falls under the definition of "Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction".
